Rafael N. Okonkwo
School of Economics, Bramwell College London
30 Tavistock Square, London WC1H 9QU
rafael.okonkwo@bramwell.ac.uk | +44 20 7946 0958 | linkedin.com/in/rafael-okonkwo
London, United Kingdom

Employment History
2020–present Senior Lecturer in Economics, Bramwell College London
2016–2020 Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Institute for Fiscal Studies, London
2015–2016 Visiting Scholar, MIT Department of Economics, Cambridge, MA

Education
2015 Ph.D. in Economics, London School of Economics
Thesis: Essays on Labour Market Frictions and Wage Dynamics
Examiners: Prof. Alan Manning, Prof. Barbara Petrongolo
2010 M.Sc. Econometrics and Mathematical Economics, London School of Economics, Distinction
2009 B.Sc. Economics, University of Cape Town, First Class Honours

Research Publications

Journal Articles
1. Okonkwo, Rafael N., and Priya Raghunathan. 2024. "Monopsony Power and the Minimum Wage: Evidence from UK Payroll Microdata." Review of Economic Studies 91(4): 2210–2247. doi:10.1093/restud/rdae019.
2. Okonkwo, Rafael N. 2022. "Search Frictions and the Gender Wage Gap in Low-Pay Labour Markets." American Economic Journal: Applied Economics 14(3): 188–215. doi:10.1257/app.20200563.
3. Hartmann, Lukas, Rafael N. Okonkwo, and Mei-Ling Tsai. 2019. "Apprenticeship Returns over the Business Cycle." Labour Economics 58: 102–117. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.labeco.2019.04.002.

Rafael N. Okonkwo page 2 of 3

Conference Proceedings
4. Okonkwo, Rafael N. 2022. "Wage Posting versus Bargaining: Field Evidence from Online Job Boards." Proceedings of the Royal Economic Society Annual Conference, University of Warwick.

Books
5. Okonkwo, Rafael N. 2023. The Frictional Economy: Why Labour Markets Fail Workers. Bristol: Bristol University Press.

Teaching
EC312 Labour Economics (BSc, 240 students), Bramwell College London, 2020–present
EC940 Advanced Econometrics (PhD core sequence), Bramwell College London, 2021–present

Awards and Grants
ESRC New Investigator Grant ES/T009315/1 (£298,540), 2021–2024
Royal Economic Society Junior Prize for best paper by an early-career economist, 2019
T.W. Schultz Memorial Prize, shortlisted, 2023

Languages
English (native), Igbo (native), French (B2 working proficiency)
Empirical toolkit: Stata, R, Python, and large administrative payroll datasets
